problem_text: Determine whether integral_1^infinity 1/x dx converges or diverges.

content.label: Improper: infinite upper limit
content.applies_when: the upper limit is infinite, so the integral is defined as a limit of proper integrals.

content.meaning: integral_a^infinity f dx = lim_{t->infinity} integral_a^t f dx.

content.label: rewrite integral_1^infinity 1/x dx = lim_{t->infinity} integral_1^t 1/x dx
content.action: rewrite integral_1^infinity 1/x dx = lim_{t->infinity} integral_1^t 1/x dx
content.purpose: convert the improper integral into a limit of proper integrals

content.label: the antiderivative of 1/x is ln x, so integral_1^t = [ln x]_1^t = ln t - 0
content.action: the antiderivative of 1/x is ln x, so integral_1^t = [ln x]_1^t = ln t - 0
content.purpose: evaluate the proper integral up to the cutoff t

content.label: let t -> infinity: ln t -> infinity, so the limit does not exist (is infinite)
content.action: let t -> infinity: ln t -> infinity, so the limit does not exist (is infinite)
content.purpose: resolve the limit and see it fails to be finite

content.label: p-integral at infinity diverges at the boundary p=1
content.applies_when: even though 1/x -> 0, the tail integral has p = 1, the borderline case
content.transformation: p = 1 => the p-integral diverges (the integrand tending to 0 is not sufficient)
